name: Pop Tarts
recipeInstructions:
Cream together sugar and vegetable shortening until light. Beat in eggs until creamy, then stir in flour and baking powder to form a soft dough. Divide dough evenly into 16 parts. Roll each part into a flattened ball and set aside on a plate. Wrap the plate in plastic wrap and refrigerate for two hours.
Grease a cookie sheet and set aside.
Roll one ball of dough into a rectangle about 1/16 inch thick. Spread a heaping teaspoon (or more) of filling evenly over the dough, leaving a generous margin. Roll another ball of dough into a similar shape and lay it neatly over the first. Using a sharp knife, trim the tart to approximately 3 1/2" x 5 inches and crimp the edge with your fingertips or the tines of a fork. Place the tart on the cookie sheet and prick the top with a fork.
Optional: Lightly beat egg white with milk; brush the tops of the tarts and sprinkle with sugar, if desired.
Bake at 350°F for 25 minutes, until edges are browned. Prick again during the first 10 minutes of baking to keep them flat. Cool, wrap, and store at room temperature or in the refrigerator. Tarts may also be iced.
